title: Luscious Crisp Moroccan Fennel Tagine
description: A fragrant, low-sodium tagine with crisp fennel, sweet apricots, and toasted almonds, served over warm double‑rise bread.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a heavy skillet over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic, cumin, coriander, and smoked paprika, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Place the sliced fennel into the skillet, tossing to coat with the spice mixture.
Add the chopped apricots, tomato paste, and lemon juice, stirring to combine.
Pour in the low-sodium chicken broth and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and let the tagine cook for 30 minutes until the fennel is tender and the flavors meld.
Stir in the toasted almond slivers just before serving to maintain their crunch.
Serve the tagine hot over freshly baked double‑rise bread, or spoon into a traditional tangia pot for an extra rustic touch.
Garnish with a sprinkle of fresh mint if desired.
